title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Bruschetta al Pomodoro
description: Classic Tuscan starter featuring toasted bread topped with fresh tomatoes, basil, and garlic. A simple yet flavourful dish that highlights the quality of summer produce.

steps.instruction: Slice the ciabatta into 1 cm thick slices and toast in the oven at 180°C for 5 minutes until golden.
Dice the tomatoes and place in a bowl. Finely chop the basil and mince the garlic. Add both to the tomatoes along with olive oil, salt, and black pepper. Mix gently.
Spoon the tomato mixture onto the toasted bread slices and serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: A light Pinot Grigio complements the fresh flavours.
tips: Use the ripest tomatoes for the best flavour
Toast the bread just before serving to keep it crisp

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy risotto infused with wild mushrooms and Parmesan cheese. A comforting dish that brings the earthy flavours of autumn to the table.

steps.instruction: Clean the mushrooms and slice them. Finely chop the shallots and mince the garlic.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large pan. Sauté the shallots and garlic for 3 minutes until soft.
Add the mushrooms and cook for 5 minutes until they release their moisture and start to brown.
Add the rice and stir for 2 minutes to toast lightly. Pour in the wine and let it evaporate completely.
Start adding the hot stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently. Wait until each addition is absorbed before adding the next. This should take about 20 minutes.
When the rice is al dente, stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan. Adjust seasoning with salt and pepper. Serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: A full-bodied white wine like Gavi di Gavi pairs beautifully.
tips: Stir constantly to release the starch and create a creamy texture
Use a good quality stock for the best flavour

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Saltimbocca alla Romana
description: A classic Roman dish of veal cutlets topped with prosciutto and sage, cooked in white wine butter sauce. Quick to prepare but packed with flavour.

steps.instruction: Lay the veal cutlets between cling film and pound them thin with a meat mallet.
Place a slice of prosciutto and 2 sage leaves on each cutlet. Roll up and secure with a toothpick.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a frying pan. Brown the saltimbocca on all sides for 2-3 minutes total. Remove from pan and set aside.
Add the remaining butter to the pan. Pour in the wine and let it reduce by half. Return the saltimbocca to the pan and cook for another minute.
Serve immediately with a drizzle of the sauce.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied red like Montepulciano d'Abruzzo is ideal.
tips: Use very thin veal cutlets for best results
Don't overcook the veal to keep it tender

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Pasta alla Norma
description: A beloved Sicilian pasta dish featuring fried aubergines, ricotta salata, and a simple tomato sauce. The combination of textures and flavours is simply divine.

steps.instruction: Dice the aubergines and toss with 10g salt. Let sit for 10 minutes then pat dry with kitchen paper.
Fry the aubergines in 60ml olive oil in batches until golden brown. Drain on kitchen paper and sprinkle with salt.
Blanch the tomatoes in boiling water for 30 seconds, then peel and chop. Crush the garlic.
Heat remaining oil in a pan. Sauté the garlic for 1 minute then add the tomatoes and red pepper flakes. Simmer for 10 minutes until thickened.
Cook the pasta in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 100ml pasta water then drain.
Toss the pasta with the tomato sauce, adding pasta water as needed. Stir in the aubergines and basil. Serve with grated ricotta salata on top.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied Sicilian red like Nero d'Avola pairs perfectly.
tips: Salt the aubergines to remove bitterness and improve texture
Fry the aubergines in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Tiramisu Classico
description: The iconic Italian dessert of coffee-soaked ladyfingers layered with mascarpone cream and dusted with cocoa. A perfect end to any meal.

steps.instruction: Brew the coffee and let it cool. Whisk the egg yolks with 60g sugar until pale and thick. Add the mascarpone and mix until smooth.
Beat the egg whites with the remaining sugar until stiff peaks form. Fold gently into the mascarpone mixture.
Quickly dip each ladyfinger in the coffee (do not soak) and arrange a layer in a dish. Sprinkle with amaretto if using.
Spread half the mascarpone cream over the ladyfingers. Repeat with another layer of dipped ladyfingers and cream.
Chill in the fridge for at least 4 hours, preferably overnight. Before serving, dust generously with cocoa powder and grate dark chocolate on top.

wine_pairing_notes: A sweet Vin Santo or espresso are traditional pairings.
tips: Use room temperature eggs for easier whisking
Chill for as long as possible for best texture

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Caprese Salad
description: A classic Italian starter highlighting the freshness of summer produce. Simple yet bursting with flavour, it's a celebration of ripe tomatoes, creamy mozzarella, and fragrant basil.

steps.instruction: Slice the tomatoes and mozzarella into 1cm thick rounds. Arrange them alternately on a platter, slightly overlapping.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the tomato and mozzarella slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Sprinkle with sea salt and black pepper.

wine_pairing_notes: A light Pinot Grigio complements the fresh flavours.
tips: Use the ripest tomatoes for the best taste. Avoid overhandling the ingredients.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Spaghetti alla Carbonara
description: Rome's iconic pasta dish made with eggs, Pecorino Romano, guanciale, and black pepper. Rich, creamy, and deeply flavourful without any cream.

steps.instruction: Cook spaghetti in salted boiling water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a pan, fry diced guanciale on medium heat until crispy. Remove from heat and set aside.
In a bowl, whisk eggs with grated Pecorino Romano and black pepper.
Add drained spaghetti to the pan with guanciale. Remove from heat, then quickly stir in the egg mixture, adding reserved pasta water as needed to create a creamy sauce.

wine_pairing_notes: A bold red like Montepulciano d'Abruzzo pairs excellently.
tips: Use room temperature eggs to prevent scrambling. Do not let the egg mixture sit in the pan too long after mixing.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Risotto ai Funghi
description: A luxurious risotto made with wild mushrooms, Arborio rice, white wine, and Parmesan. Creamy, earthy, and deeply satisfying, perfect for a cosy dinner.

steps.instruction: Finely chop the mushrooms. Finely dice the shallots and mince the garlic.
In a wide pan, heat olive oil and 10g butter over medium heat. Sauté shallots and garlic until soft.
Add chopped mushrooms and thyme, cooking until they release their moisture and turn golden. Season with salt and pepper.
Add Arborio rice, stirring to coat with oil and mushrooms. Pour in white wine and cook until absorbed.
Add hot vegetable stock one ladle at a time, stirring constantly until each addition is absorbed before adding the next (approx. 18-20 minutes).
Remove from heat, stir in remaining butter and grated Parmesan. Season to taste and serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied white like Verdicchio pairs beautifully.
tips: Stir frequently for a creamy texture. Use homemade stock for deeper flavour. Add stock gradually to prevent a soupy risotto.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Melanzane alla Parmigiana
description: A baked dish of fried aubergine layered with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan. Hearty, cheesy, and full of Mediterranean flavours.

steps.instruction: Slice aubergines into 1cm thick rounds. Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 20 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry.
Beat eggs in a bowl. Place flour in another bowl. Dip aubergine slices in flour, then egg.
Heat olive oil in a deep pan. Fry aubergine slices in batches until golden. Drain on kitchen paper.
In a saucepan, heat butter and sauté garlic. Add passata, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes.
Layer half the aubergine slices in a baking dish. Top with half the tomato sauce, mozzarella, Parmesan, and basil. Repeat layers.
Bake at 180°C for 25 minutes until golden and bubbling. Let rest for 10 minutes before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: A full-bodied red like Nero d'Avola complements the richness.
tips: Salt the aubergines well to remove bitterness. Fry in batches to avoid overcrowding the pan. Use fresh basil for the best flavour.

title: [EDGE-no-nutrition] Tiramisu
description: Italy's most famous dessert, featuring layers of coffee-soaked ladyfingers and mascarpone cream. Light, creamy, and utterly indulgent.

steps.instruction: Prepare coffee and let it cool. If using, mix brandy into coffee.
Separate egg whites from yolks. Whisk yolks with 60g sugar until pale and thick. Add mascarpone and whisk until smooth.
Beat egg whites with remaining sugar until stiff peaks form. Gently fold into mascarpone mixture.
Dip ladyfingers briefly in coffee and arrange a layer in a dish. Cover with a layer of mascarpone cream.
Repeat layers. Dust top with cocoa powder. Chill for at least 4 hours before serving. Shave dark chocolate on top if desired.

wine_pairing_notes: A sweet wine like Vin Santo pairs well.
tips: Use fresh eggs for the best texture. Chill the tiramisu well before serving. Avoid soaking the ladyfingers too long to prevent a soggy texture.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Spaghetti Aglio e Olio
description: A classic Roman dish with a simple yet flavorful combination of garlic, olive oil, and chili. Quick to prepare and perfect for a light meal.

steps.instruction: Cook the spaghetti in a large pot of boiling salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
In a large pan, heat the olive oil over low heat. Add the minced garlic and chili flakes, sautéing until the garlic is golden but not browned.
Add the drained spaghetti to the pan and toss to coat in the garlic oil. Add a splash of reserved pasta water to emulsify the sauce.
Season with salt to taste, stir in the chopped parsley, and serve immediately.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a light Pinot Grigio to complement the garlic and chili flavors.
tips: Use the best quality olive oil you can afford for the best flavor.
Don't overcook the garlic to avoid bitterness.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Risotto ai Funghi
description: A creamy risotto featuring fresh wild mushrooms and Parmesan cheese. Rich and satisfying, perfect for a special occasion.

steps.instruction: Finely chop the onion, garlic, and mushrooms. Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large pan over medium heat. Add onion and garlic, sauté until softened.
Add the mushrooms and rosemary, cooking until the mushrooms release their moisture and it evaporates. Season with salt and pepper.
Add the risotto rice to the pan, stirring to coat the grains with oil. Pour in the white wine and cook until fully absorbed.
Gradually add the hot vegetable stock, one ladle at a time, stirring frequently and allowing the liquid to absorb before adding more. This should take about 16-18 minutes.
Once the rice is creamy and al dente, remove from heat. Stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan cheese. Adjust seasoning if needed. Cover and let rest for 2 minutes before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a full-bodied white wine such as a Chardonnay to match the creamy texture and umami flavors.
tips: Use a good quality vegetable stock for the best flavor.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Melanzane alla Parmigiana
description: Layers of fried aubergine, tomato sauce, and melted cheese. A comforting dish that brings together the flavors of Southern Italy.

steps.instruction: Slice the aubergines into 1 cm thick rounds. Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 20 minutes to draw out excess moisture. Pat dry with paper towels.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Fry the aubergine slices in batches until golden brown on both sides, about 3-4 minutes per side. Drain on paper towels.
In a separate pan, sauté finely chopped onion and garlic in a little olive oil until softened. Add the tomato passata, season with salt and pepper, and simmer for 10 minutes. Stir in chopped basil.
Preheat the oven to 190°C (375°F). In a baking dish, layer half the fried aubergine slices, half the tomato sauce, half the mozzarella, and a sprinkle of Parmesan. Repeat the layers.
Bake for 25-30 minutes until the top is golden and bubbling. Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied red wine such as a Chianti to complement the tomato and cheese flavors.
tips: Slicing the aubergine thinly ensures even cooking and a better texture.
You can prepare the dish ahead and bake it just before serving for convenience.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Pasta alla Norma
description: A Sicilian classic featuring pasta with a rich tomato and aubergine sauce, topped with ricotta salata. Simple yet bursting with flavor.

steps.instruction: Cut the aubergines into 1 cm cubes. Sprinkle with salt and let sit for 15 minutes to draw out moisture. Pat dry with paper towels.
Heat olive oil in a large pan over medium heat. Fry the aubergine cubes in batches until golden brown. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté finely chopped onion and garlic until softened. Add the tomato passata, season with salt and pepper, and cook for 10 minutes.
Add the fried aubergine and chopped basil to the tomato sauce. Cook for another 5 minutes to meld the flavors.
Cook the pasta in a large pot of boiling salted water until al dente. Reserve 1 cup of pasta water before draining.
Toss the drained pasta with the aubergine sauce, adding a splash of reserved pasta water if needed. Serve topped with grated ricotta salata.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied red wine such as a Nero d'Avola to complement the rich tomato and aubergine flavors.
tips: Do not skip salting the aubergine to remove excess moisture, which can make the dish watery.
Use a good quality passata for a richer tomato flavor.

title: [EDGE-sparse] Focaccia Genovese
description: A traditional Genoese focaccia with a soft, fluffy interior and a crispy, olive oil-kissed crust. Perfect as an appetizer or snack.

steps.instruction: In a small bowl, dissolve the yeast and sugar in warm water. Let sit for 5 minutes until frothy.
In a large bowl, mix the flour and salt. Make a well in the center and pour in the yeast mixture and olive oil. Stir until a shaggy dough forms.
Turn the dough out onto a lightly floured surface and knead for 10 minutes until smooth and elastic. Place in a lightly oiled bowl, cover, and let rise for 1 hour or until doubled in size.
Preheat the oven to 220°C (425°F). Grease a 20x30 cm baking tray with olive oil. Spread the dough out in the tray, pressing it to the edges. Dimple the dough with your fingertips.
Drizzle generously with olive oil, sprinkle with sea salt flakes and rosemary (if using). Let rise for another 20 minutes.
Bake for 20-25 minutes until golden brown. Serve warm or at room temperature.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a light sparkling wine such as Prosecco to complement the crispy texture and olive oil.
tips: For extra flavor, add a few pitted olives or cherry tomatoes on top before baking.
The dough should be sticky; resist the urge to add too much extra flour.

description: A fresh and peppery pesto made with rocket (arugula) instead of basil, tossed with pasta. Quick and vibrant, perfect for summer.

title: [EDGE-contradictory] Pollo alla Cacciatora
description: A rustic Tuscan stew of chicken braised with tomatoes, olives, and herbs. Hearty and comforting, ideal for cooler days.

steps.instruction: Chop onion, carrots, celery, and garlic finely. Heat olive oil in a large pan. Season chicken with salt and pepper, then brown on all sides. Remove and set aside.
In the same pan, sauté onion, carrots, celery, and garlic until softened. Add rosemary and black olives, cook 2 minutes.
Pour in white wine, let it reduce by half. Add chopped tomatoes, bring to a simmer.
Return chicken to the pan, cover, and simmer for 30 minutes until chicken is tender and sauce thickens.
Serve hot with crusty bread or polenta to soak up the sauce.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a medium-bodied red like Chianti or Sangiovese.
tips: Use bone-in, skin-on chicken thighs for the best flavor and texture.

tips: Use high-quality olive oil for the best flavor.
Don’t overcook the garlic to avoid bitterness.
embedding_text: Spaghetti Aglio e Olio A simple yet flavorful dish that highlights the beauty of minimal ingredients. It's quick to prepare and bursting with garlic and olive oil essence. spaghetti garlic olive oil chilli flakes parsley salt black pepper savoury garlicky Italian spring summer autumn winter

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Eggplant Parmigiana
description: A layered, baked delight featuring crispy fried aubergine and rich tomato sauce. This dish is a hearty vegetarian classic.

steps.instruction: Slice the aubergine into 1cm thick rounds. Salt both sides and let sit for 20 minutes to remove bitterness. Pat dry with kitchen paper.
Set up three bowls: one with flour, one with beaten eggs, and one with breadcrumbs. Bread each aubergine slice.
Heat olive oil in a pan over medium heat. Fry aubergine slices until golden on both sides (about 3 minutes per side). Drain on paper towels.
In a saucepan, heat olive oil and sauté garlic until fragrant. Add tomato passata, oregano, salt, and pepper. Simmer for 10 minutes.
In a baking dish, layer aubergine slices with tomato sauce, mozzarella, and Parmesan. Repeat layers, finishing with cheese on top.
Bake in a preheated oven at 190°C for 25 minutes or until bubbly and golden. Garnish with fresh basil before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: A medium-bodied Italian red like Chianti or Sangiovese pairs beautifully with the rich, cheesy layers.
tips: For extra crispiness, double-fry the aubergine slices.
Let the dish rest for 10 minutes after baking to help it set before serving.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Risotto ai Funghi
description: Creamy Arborio rice cooked with wild mushrooms and white wine for an umami-rich dish. This risotto is comforting and perfect for autumn evenings.

steps.instruction: Finely chop shallots and garlic. Clean mushrooms and slice into thin pieces.
Heat olive oil and half the butter in a large pan over medium heat. Sauté shallots and garlic until translucent (3 minutes).
Add mushrooms and thyme, cooking until mushrooms release their moisture and it evaporates (8 minutes).
Add Arborio rice to the pan, stirring to coat the grains with oil. Pour in the white wine and cook until fully absorbed.
Add heated stock one ladle at a time, stirring frequently until the liquid is absorbed before adding more (about 20 minutes total).
Once the rice is creamy and al dente, stir in the remaining butter and Parmesan. Season with salt and pepper to taste.

wine_pairing_notes: A full-bodied white wine like Gavi or a light red like Pinot Noir complements the earthy mushrooms.
tips: Use a good-quality Parmesan for the best flavor.
Keep the stock warm in a separate pan to maintain a consistent cooking temperature.

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Caprese Salad
description: A fresh and vibrant salad with ripe tomatoes, creamy mozzarella, and fragrant basil. Simple yet iconic, perfect for summer.

steps.instruction: Slice tomatoes and mozzarella into 1cm thick rounds. Arrange alternately on a plate.
Tuck fresh basil leaves between the tomato and mozzarella slices.
Drizzle with olive oil and balsamic vinegar. Season with salt and black pepper to taste.

wine_pairing_notes: A crisp Italian white like Vermentino or a light rosé pairs perfectly with the fresh flavors.
tips: Use the ripest tomatoes you can find for the best flavor.
Drizzle balsamic vinegar sparingly to avoid overpowering the salad.
embedding_text: Caprese Salad A fresh and vibrant salad with ripe tomatoes, creamy mozzarella, and fragrant basil. Simple yet iconic, perfect for summer. tomatoes mozzarella basil olive oil balsamic vinegar salt black pepper fresh herbaceous savoury Italian summer

title: [EDGE-long-cook] Tiramisu
description: A classic Italian dessert featuring layers of coffee-soaked sponge and mascarpone cream, dusted with cocoa. Decadent and delightful.

steps.instruction: Brew the coffee and let it cool. If using rum or Marsala, mix into the coffee.
Separate egg whites from yolks. Whisk yolks with 80g sugar until pale and thick (about 5 minutes).
In another bowl, beat egg whites with remaining sugar until stiff peaks form (about 5 minutes).
Gently fold mascarpone into the egg yolk mixture until smooth. Fold in egg whites until fully combined.
Dip each ladyfinger briefly into the coffee (just enough to moisten, not soak) and arrange in a single layer in a dish.
Spread half the mascarpone mixture over the ladyfingers. Repeat with another layer of coffee-dipped ladyfingers and remaining cream.
Grate dark chocolate over the top and dust generously with cocoa powder. Chill for at least 4 hours (or overnight) before serving.

wine_pairing_notes: Pair with a sweet dessert wine like Vin Santo or a coffee liqueur to enhance the coffee flavors.
tips: Use high-quality coffee for the best flavor.
Let the tiramisu chill for at least 4 hours to allow flavors to meld.
